About Shiela

Senior Amazon creative strategy with marketplace execution behind it.

I'm Shiela Mae Rivera, an Amazon Growth Creative Strategist specializing in PDP image systems, Premium A+ Content, Brand Story, Storefront architecture, and catalog-scale Amazon content operations.

My work sits between strategy and execution. I do not approach Amazon pages as isolated graphics. I build structured content systems that help shoppers understand value quickly, help brands maintain consistency across ASINs, and help marketplace teams move faster without sacrificing quality.

Across agency, distributor, and founder-led brand environments, I have supported Amazon content for wellness, beauty, home, lifestyle, baby, pet, craft, and consumer product brands. That experience gives me a practical view of how Amazon creative decisions affect shopper clarity, catalog consistency, launch readiness, and conversion-focused merchandising.

9+ Years Amazon marketplace experience
200+ Amazon brands and content systems supported
40K+ ASIN catalog exposure across complex workflows
What I Fix

Most Amazon content problems are not design problems alone.

A listing can look visually polished while still underperforming. If the page does not help shoppers quickly understand the product, compare options confidently, and move through the buying decision without friction, the content is limiting performance regardless of how it looks.

Problem 01

Image galleries without decision flow

The images hold up individually, but the sequence does not answer the shopper's questions in the right order.

Problem 02

Premium A+ that repeats bullet points

The A+ section restates features instead of deepening trust, explaining value, and handling objections.

Problem 03

Storefronts that behave like brochures

The storefront shows the brand but does not guide shoppers into the right product path or category decision.

Problem 04

Paid traffic sent to weak PDPs

Traffic can reach the listing, but weak PDP content fails to communicate value quickly enough to support conversion.

Problem 05

Catalog inconsistency across ASINs

Different layouts, copy styles, and visual standards make the brand feel less established than it actually is.

Problem 06

Creative workflows that cannot scale

Without briefs, rules, templates, and systematic processes, teams produce slower work with more revision cycles.

I do not build isolated Amazon graphics. I build Amazon content systems.

What makes an Amazon page convert?

Strong Amazon pages reduce friction, increase understanding, improve trust, and guide shoppers toward purchase confidence.

◉

3-Second Clarity

Customers should immediately understand the product, purpose, and value proposition — before they scroll.

◎

USP Communication

Core differentiators should be visually communicated instead of hidden inside dense copy that shoppers skip.

✦

Premium A+

Premium A+ should function like a structured conversion system — not decorative filler after the bullet points.

↗

PDP Image Stack

Gallery systems should strategically handle objections, use cases, trust signals, specs, and comparison flow.

▣

Storefront UX

Navigation and merchandising hierarchy should improve product discovery, cross-sell, and catalog cohesion.

$

Conversion Messaging

Messaging should improve comprehension, reduce uncertainty, and support confident buying decisions on mobile.

How it works

A structured process built for clarity, conversion, and Amazon implementation readiness.

Step 1

Audit the ecosystem

Review PDPs, galleries, Brand Story, Storefront structure, shopper flow, mobile readability, messaging clarity, and catalog inconsistencies before touching a single asset.

Step 2

Build the strategy

Define hierarchy, USP communication, module structure, objection handling, and conversion direction before production begins.

Step 3

Create the content system

Develop image stacks, Premium A+, Brand Story modules, and Amazon-ready creative structures built around real shopper behavior.

Step 4

Deliver organized assets

Exported files, source assets, implementation notes, compliance guidance, and handoff documentation — ready for your team or account manager.

Here is what you get.

Amazon Content Audit

Visual hierarchy review, conversion gaps, mobile readability check, and actionable recommendations.

PDP Image Gallery System

Structured 7–9 image strategy for benefits, objections, use cases, specs, and comparison flow.

Premium A+ Architecture

Module sequence planning, copy structure direction, and mobile-first readability optimization.

Brand Story Framework

Reusable Amazon brand narrative aligned with the PDP ecosystem and catalog standards.

Storefront UX Strategy

Navigation structure, merchandising hierarchy, cross-sell logic, and seasonal collection planning.

Production Handoff

Exported assets, organized source files, designer briefs, and implementation guidance.

Amazon Content Systems

The work isn't just individual assets. I build reusable content structures that scale.

This is where the experience is strongest: translating product value, customer objections, review language, category expectations, and brand positioning into systems that work across one hero ASIN or a large catalog.

PDP Flow

Image gallery sequencing

Mapping the role of each image so the page answers the shopper's questions in a logical buying order — not just a visual parade.

A+ Content

Module purpose and hierarchy

Structuring Premium A+ around trust, education, comparison, differentiation, and brand credibility — not repeated bullet points.

Brand Story

Cross-ASIN brand trust

Using Brand Story to strengthen the brand experience consistently across listings — not to repeat generic company language.

Catalog

Variation and comparison logic

Creating consistent ways to explain sizes, colors, bundles, features, and product families across ASINs at scale.

Storefront

Shopper navigation paths

Organizing Storefronts around how customers browse, compare, and choose across categories — not how the brand organizes its own SKUs.

Operations

Briefs and AI-assisted workflows

Turning content decisions into repeatable briefs, QA rules, and prompt-supported workflows that help teams produce faster without losing quality.

Why Work With Me

Senior Amazon content strategy with direct execution, faster communication, and marketplace-native judgment.

Traditional agencies are valuable when brands need full operational support across PPC, logistics, account management, and backend systems. My role is more specialized: I focus on the customer-facing content layer that directly affects shopper trust, PDP clarity, merchandising consistency, and conversion support across Amazon listings, Premium A+, Brand Story, and Storefronts.

Capability Traditional Agency General Freelancer Working With Me
Amazon PDP conversion judgment Often available, but spread across account, copy, and design teams. Usually inconsistent unless they specialize deeply in Amazon. Direct Amazon-native judgment from strategy through execution.
Premium A+ and Brand Story structure Often templated or production-driven. Often visual-first with limited module strategy. Built around shopper education, trust, comparison, and brand credibility.
Catalog consistency Can be strong, but may require more internal coordination. Often hard to maintain across many ASINs. A core strength from large catalog and agency environments.
Communication speed May pass through account managers and multiple production layers. Can be fast, but may need heavy direction. Direct communication with the person planning and executing the content.
Strategic and hands-on execution Often split between different roles. Often execution-heavy with limited strategy. Content strategy, copy direction, design execution, and workflow structure combined.
AI-assisted workflow capability Depends on internal adoption and team process. Often tool-based rather than system-based. Prompt systems, content planning, briefs, and QA logic built into the workflow.

Common Questions

Most common questions

Answers for brands, agencies, and marketplace teams considering Amazon content support.

Do you handle PPC?

No. My specialization is Amazon content systems — Premium A+, image galleries, Brand Story, and Storefront UX. I can collaborate with PPC or SEO teams when needed, but I do not manage ads. Content strategy and execution is my focus.

Do you create videos?

No video production is included. Existing video assets can be integrated strategically into the content system and Storefront where applicable.

Can you support large catalogs?

Yes. I have experience supporting catalogs ranging from focused product lines to 40,000+ ASINs, building template logic and systems that maintain quality at scale.

Can agencies hire you?

Yes. I support Amazon agencies as a senior Amazon creative strategist — providing content direction, client-ready briefs, creative audits, and overflow execution support.

Do you work on Vendor Central?

Yes. I have direct experience across both Seller Central and Vendor Central environments, including compliance requirements, A+ approval workflows, and catalog standards specific to each platform.

Are you available for full-time roles?

Yes. I am open to both consulting engagements and full-time remote opportunities with Amazon agencies, brands, and marketplace operators. Serious inquiries can contact me directly for availability and role fit.

Amazon Marketplace Intelligence

Amazon’s 2026 Visual Content Shift Is Changing How Products Compete

Amazon is no longer rewarding listings that simply “look professional.” The platform is increasingly prioritizing content that improves shopper understanding, supports AI-assisted product discovery, and reduces buying hesitation across mobile devices.

Amazon’s ecosystem is becoming more AI-assisted and behavior-driven. Systems like Rufus AI, OCR image scanning, semantic search understanding, and conversational shopping are changing how products are surfaced and recommended to customers.

This means your visual content is no longer just design. It is now part of Amazon’s discovery and conversion infrastructure.

Amazon’s AI systems can now interpret image overlay text, shopper intent, semantic language, customer questions, comparison structures, visual hierarchy, and use-case clarity.

Brands still relying on crowded infographic layouts, unreadable mobile text, weak comparison visuals, inconsistent branding, and outdated Premium A+ structures are becoming easier to outrank.

Most Amazon purchases now happen on mobile, where customers scan instead of read. Amazon’s current content direction increasingly favors faster comprehension, stronger product clarity, objection-reduction visuals, and structured AI-readable content.

Compliance risk is also becoming a larger issue. Amazon’s automated enforcement systems are becoming stricter across image overlays, health claims, comparison messaging, and backend content structures.

Many brands are unknowingly losing conversion because their galleries fail mobile readability tests, Brand Story modules are underutilized, storefronts lack merchandising flow, and Premium A+ remains decorative instead of strategic.

This is why many founder-led brands are rebuilding their Amazon visual content systems before Prime Day, Q4, catalog expansion, and major product launches.

Strategic Amazon Content Audit

Modern Amazon content systems now directly influence discoverability, shopper trust, and conversion performance.

I help founder-led Amazon brands modernize image galleries, Premium A+, Brand Story modules, and storefront architecture using conversion-focused systems built for today’s AI-assisted marketplace behavior.

If your Amazon content has not been strategically updated within the last 12–18 months, there is a strong chance your catalog is already falling behind how Amazon shoppers and Amazon’s AI systems now evaluate product pages.
